title: jasione sessiliflora subsp. tomentosa
intro: Jasione sessiliflora subsp. tomentosa, also known as woolly bellflower or hairy bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Jasione sessiliflora subsp. tomentosa include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the flowers and leaves in hot water or oil, which can be used as a topical application or ingested in small amounts to reap its medicinal benefits.
